title: Assignment 3
description: Principal components analysis, agglomerative hierarchical clustering, Shiny and website update.
contents:
Assignment 3 - ESM 244 (Winter 2022)
Due Tuesday, 2022-02-22 at 5:00pm PST
Task 1, PCA: submit on Gauchospace (10 points)
Task 2, Hierarchical clustering: submit on Gauchospace (10 points)
Task 3, update personal website (5 points) (update link to your site HERE if you change your repo name!)
Task 4, update Shiny app (5 points)
Task 5, choose a time slot for presenting your Shiny app

title: Assignment 4
description: Spatial data visualization, time series, text wrangling and analysis, Shiny and website update.
contents:
Assignment 4 - ESM 244 (Winter 2022)
Due Monday, March 14, 2022 by 5pm.
Task 1: Spatial data visualization: 10 pts; Submit to Gauchospace
Task 2: Willamette Falls time series: 20 pts; Submit to Gauchospace
Task 3: Text wrangling and analysis: 10 pts; Submit to Gauchospace
Task 4: Keep finalizing your personal website
Task 5: Prepare to present your Shiny app

title: Assignment 2
description: Binary logistic regression, non-linear least squares, Shiny and website update.
contents:
Assignment 2 - ESM 244 (Winter 2022)
Due Monday 2022-02-07 at 5:00pm PST
Task 1 and 2: Upload knitted HTML to GauchoSpace (10 pts each)
Task 3: Add your Github repository URL to the Shiny App Information Page. If your repository is private, add Casey (GitHub: oharac) as a collaborator to your Shiny app repo. (5 pts)
Task 4: We should already have your website link (but update here if it’s changed) (5 pts)

title: Assignment 1
description: Data wrangling & viz, model selection, Reprex, Shiny app sign-ups, build your skeleton website.
contents:
Assignment 1 - ESM 244 (Winter 2022)
All parts due by 5pm PST on Monday 1/24/2022
Tasks 1 & 2: Submit individual knitted HTML for Tasks 1 & 2 through GauchoSpace
Task 3: post working and broken reprexes to Slack and Github (instructions HERE)
Task 4: Add your Shiny app information HERE (one entry per group)
Task 5: Start building your personal website in R with {distill} and {postcards}
